```{r}

# First, create the binned sequence variable
mdf <- mdf |>
  mutate(seq_bin = cut(seq.fp, 
                       breaks = c(-Inf, 0.3, 0.7, Inf),
                       labels = c("g1", "g2", "g3")))

# Piecewise versions of the models

# random intercept
m1_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + (1 | participant), 
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# fixed slope, random intercept
m2_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + seq.fp * seq_bin + (1 | participant), 
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# random slope, random intercept
m3_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + seq.fp * seq_bin + (1 + seq.fp | participant), 
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# quadratic random slope, random intercept
m4_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + seq.fp * seq_bin + I(seq.fp^2) * seq_bin + 
                (1 + seq.fp + I(seq.fp^2) | participant),
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# log-transformed time; fixed slope, random intercept
m5_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + log(seq.fp + 1) * seq_bin + (1 | participant), 
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# log-transformed time; random slope, random intercept
m6_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + log(seq.fp + 1) * seq_bin + 
                (1 + log(seq.fp + 1) | participant),
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

# linear and log-transformed time; fixed slope, random intercept
m7_pw <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + seq.fp * seq_bin + log(seq.fp + 1) * seq_bin + 
                (1 + seq.fp + log(seq.fp + 1) | participant),
              data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

compare_performance(m1_pw, m2_pw, m3_pw, m4_pw, m5_pw, m6_pw, m7_pw, rank = T)
compare_performance(m6_pw, m7, metrics = "common", rank = F)
```

```{r}
# 1. Create a new dataset for predictions
new_data <- expand.grid(
  seq.fp = seq(0, 1, length.out = 100),
  participant = unique(mdf$participant)[1]  # Use the first participant for predictions
)

# Add seq_bin to the new data
new_data <- new_data |>
  mutate(seq_bin = cut(seq.fp, 
                       breaks = c(-Inf, 0.3, 0.7, Inf),
                       labels = c("g1", "g2", "g3")))

# 2. Generate predictions
predictions <- predict(m6_pw, newdata = new_data, re.form = NA)  # Marginal predictions

# 3. Add predictions to the new_data
new_data$predicted_tct <- predictions

# 4. Plot using ggplot2
ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = mdf, aes(x = seq.fp, y = tct), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(data = new_data, aes(x = seq.fp, y = predicted_tct), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.3, 0.7), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Standardized Task Sequence (SC)",
       y = "Task Completion Time (seconds)",
       title = "Predicted Learning Curve from Piecewise Model") +
  theme_minimal()


```

```{r}
m6_pw_treat <- lmer(tct ~ 1 + log(seq.fp + 1) * seq_bin * treatment + 
                      (1 + log(seq.fp + 1) | participant),
                    data = mdf, REML = FALSE)

new_data <- expand.grid(
  seq.fp = seq(0, 1, length.out = 100),
  treatment = unique(mdf$treatment),
  participant = unique(mdf$participant)[1]  # Use the first participant for predictions
)

new_data <- new_data |>
  mutate(seq_bin = cut(seq.fp, 
                       breaks = c(-Inf, 0.3, 0.7, Inf),
                       labels = c("g1", "g2", "g3")))

# Generate predictions
predictions <- predict(m6_pw_treat, newdata = new_data, re.form = NA)  # Marginal predictions

# Add predictions to the new_data
new_data$predicted_tct <- predictions

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = mdf, aes(x = seq.fp, y = tct, color = treatment), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(data = new_data, aes(x = seq.fp, y = predicted_tct, color = treatment), size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.3, 0.7), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Standardized Task Sequence (SC)",
       y = "Task Completion Time (seconds)",
       title = "Predicted Learning Curves by Treatment Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1")  # Use a color-blind friendly palette

```
